operator.note: Provocation: amputate the observable frontier and see if discovery still happens — separating 'novel information added' from 'number went up,' which the paper silently fuses.

question: Treat the leaderboard scalar as a latent variable the platform refuses to reveal: the verifier still computes the score but agents receive only a binary "admitted as a novel contribution to the record" from a meta-verifier that admits a submission iff it is (i) correct AND (ii) not information-redundant with the existing corpus — regardless of whether the hidden scalar improved. Does collective progress survive, or even improve, when "discovery" is defined as "added admissible new information to the corpus" rather than "moved the frontier"? If it survives, the scalar frontier was never the engine of discovery — only the scoreboard we mistook for it.

floor.minimal_experiment: Take a multi-agent research/solve benchmark where a ground-truth scalar EXISTS and is computable but where submissions are richly structured (e.g. the paper's MAS arena, or a program-synthesis / theorem-proving / ARC-style task suite where each submission is a solution+proof/trace). Run three arms on the same agent population, same budget, same task stream. ARM-A (control, frontier-visible): agents see the scalar and are rewarded for moving it (the paper's regime). ARM-B (treatment, scalar-hidden / novelty-admission): the verifier computes the scalar but never reveals it; agents get only a binary admit/reject from a meta-verifier that admits iff submission is correct AND its embedding/feature-vector is >epsilon away from every admitted record entry (non-redundancy gate). ARM-C (placebo control, isolates the hiding from the redundancy logic): scalar hidden, binary signal = correct-only (NO redundancy check) — this separates 'hiding the number' from 'rewarding non-redundancy'. Measured quantities, all computed on the HELD-BACK scalar the agents never saw: (1) best-achieved scalar over time, (2) corpus coverage = number of distinct admissible solution-clusters, (3) corpus quality = scalar of the best entry AND mean scalar of admitted entries, (4) redundancy rate of the visible-frontier arm's record vs the admission arm's record. Pre-register epsilon and the embedding/feature space (this is the load-bearing knob — report sensitivity across >=3 epsilon settings and >=2 representations).
floor.falsifiable_prediction: ARM-B (scalar-hidden + redundancy-admission) reaches a best-achieved hidden-scalar within a small margin of ARM-A (frontier-visible) — NOT detectably worse — while producing strictly higher corpus coverage and a lower redundancy rate than both A and C. The sharp falsifier: if ARM-B's best hidden-scalar collapses materially below ARM-A's (agents stop improving once they cannot see the number), the claim 'the scalar was never the engine' is FALSE — visibility of the frontier was load-bearing for progress. A second falsifier: if ARM-B and ARM-C (correct-only, no redundancy gate) are statistically indistinguishable on coverage and quality, then the redundancy meta-verifier added nothing and 'admissible new information' reduces to 'just hide the number,' deflating the mechanism.
floor.control: Two controls. (1) ARM-A frontier-visible regime = the status-quo scalar-driven baseline. (2) ARM-C scalar-hidden-but-correct-only = placebo that holds 'the number is hidden' constant while removing the non-redundancy logic, isolating which half of the manipulation (hiding vs. redundancy-rewarding) drives any observed effect. Both run on the same population/budget/task-stream, all evaluated on the held-back scalar.
floor.reject_reason:
[discriminating] The three-arm design is better than average — the ARM-C placebo cleanly separates "hide the number" from "reward non-redundancy," and the two named falsifiers (best-scalar collapse kills the thesis; ARM-B≈ARM-C deflates the mechanism) are aimed in opposite directions and stated in advance. But it is NOT genuinely discriminating, for three reasons.

(1) The "falsifiable prediction" is partly trivial-by-construction. ARM-B's reward IS a non-redundancy gate, while "coverage" is defined as count of distinct admissible clusters and "redundancy rate" is the complement. So predictions (ii) ARM-B has strictly higher coverage and (iii) lower redundancy than the scalar-only arms are near-tautological — you are testing your own definition, not the world. Only conjunct (i), "best hidden-scalar survives within a small margin of ARM-A," is non-trivial; all discriminating power collapses onto that one comparison.

(2) That single load-bearing comparison is dominated by a knob the author concedes is decisive but cannot canonically pin down. Per the actual paper (EinsteinArena, arXiv:2606.10402v1; Table 1), submissions are mathematical constructions — point configurations (kissing number d=11: 593→604), degree-69 polynomials, circle packings — objects with NO natural metric/embedding. Rotations/permutations leave the scalar invariant but move raw coordinates arbitrarily far; distinct constructions can embed close. So coverage, redundancy, and whether ARM-B's gate even admits the frontier-improving submission are dominated by an arbitrary epsilon/representation choice. Prescribing ">=3 epsilon and >=2 representations" makes this worse, not better: it yields a result grid in which some cell shows "survives" and some "collapses," enabling post-hoc selection rather than a single committed test.

(3) The treatment confounds the very hypotheses it claims to separate. ARM-A rewards moving the scalar; ARM-B causally screens the scalar off from reward and pays only for correct+novel. The paper's actual frontier gains (11 of 12 results) came from INCREMENTAL, iterative, near-redundant submissions building on prior ones — exactly the moves a non-redundancy gate mechanically REJECTS. So if ARM-B's best-scalar collapses, the stated falsifier ("frontier visibility was load-bearing") is indistinguishable from the alternative ("the novelty gate rejected the incremental improvements that produce frontier progress"). The decisive arm produces the same observable collapse under both competing hypotheses, so it cannot adjudicate between them.

Additionally, executability is heavy though secondary: the real result required ~2 months of in-the-wild multi-agent traffic over 12 hard open-math problems with verifier refinement and a forum; running three matched-budget arms plus a defensible meta-verifier embedding for non-embeddable objects is a large, costly re-run, not a minimal experiment.

Net: the prediction is partly trivially-true, the one non-trivial comparison is governed by an admittedly decisive metric knob with no canonical setting (enabling result-agnostic self-rationalization via the sensitivity sweep), and the treatment confounds the two hypotheses on the only load-bearing arm. The experiment does not cleanly distinguish "the scalar was never the engine" from "the novelty gate destroyed the incremental mechanism that drives the scalar." refuted=true. ｜ [so_what] 两种结局都不翻转任何具体决策。(A) 若"集体进步存活/变好"→结论是"标量前沿只是记分牌不是引擎"——这只是 novelty search / MAP-Elites / Goodhart 已有文献(乃至本项目自己的 QD 引擎前提)的复述,没有任何平台运营方(Kaggle、arena、benchmark 维护者)会据此拆掉标量榜:因为可接纳门的核心"信息冗余判定(ii)"恰恰把整个难题偷换成已解——而谁来计算 information-redundancy、那个 meta-verifier 怎么造,正是本项目 MEMORY 里反复点名的真正命门(0 驳回 / LLM 自评几乎永远说 yes / 识别到 c21≡c15 却不下刀)。实验只产出"存活/死亡"这一 bit,不告诉造冗余闸门的团队该怎么造,难点被假设已解。(B) 若"进步死亡"→结论是"标量是承重的",但没人正打算拆榜,确认"该留着"同样改不了任何在投决策。点名:即便是 frameshift 自己造对抗式价值闸门的团队,拿到这个 survives/dies 结论也得不到任何门设计决策——因为问题把闸门的可实现性当前提塞了进去。这正是用户自己在 M3 诊断里判过的 c01/c02:"漂亮但实验空,把有画面感误当新意"。属于"加深对'记分牌≠引擎'这一已知框架的理解",即判据明令归为空话的那一类。

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: Builders of automated-research / agentic-discovery platforms and ML-competition/benchmark designers (e.g. Kaggle-style, AI-for-science, self-improving-agent labs) deciding whether to expose a leaderboard scalar; and meta-science researchers studying whether metric-driven competition actually causes discovery or merely correlates with it.

prior_art.known_name: Goodhart's law (metric-as-target) + Novelty Search / Quality-Diversity (Lehman & Stanley; novelty-as-objective, MAP-Elites) + surprise/information-gain-as-reward
prior_art.novelty_watermark: Known mechanisms operationalized in a new place: the contribution is not 'optimize novelty instead of objective' (that is novelty search) but the specific epistemic claim — make the objective scalar an UNOBSERVABLE latent and replace it with a binary corpus-non-redundancy admission gate, then test whether the held-out scalar still advances. The 3-arm design that separates 'hiding the number' from 'rewarding non-redundancy' is the genuinely new operationalization; the underlying levers are well-named.

lenses.reason:
机制确实独立于已收方向 [1]。两者表面都"质疑 leaderboard 标量是否是发现的真正引擎",但攻击点和可检验机制不同:

[1] (dof1) 的机制是 KILL/拒稿决策的信息价值——它把平台当 VC 漏斗,建一个"反组合"账本(被拒提案 + 被判死的问题),检验平台科学价值能否由 KILL 决策质量预测,而非排行榜胜负。它仍然保留标量分数作为背景,只是换了"用什么去预测平台价值"的预测器(KILL 质量 vs leaderboard 胜负)。其单位是"被丢弃的问题/提案",数据来自负空间。

待审问题的机制是用一个 meta-verifier 把标量分数本身从 agent 的反馈回路里彻底抹除(标量变成平台拒绝披露的潜变量),代之以二元 admit 信号 = (correct) AND (not information-redundant with corpus)。它检验的是:当"发现"被重定义为"向语料库新增可采纳信息"而非"推动 frontier"时,集体进步是否存活/更优——这是一个干预实验(ablate 标量反馈 vs 保留),其反事实判据是 corpus 信息增量,而非 KILL 决策的预测力。

可单独设计区分实验:[1] 是观测性/预测性研究(回归:KILL 质量 → 平台价值),保留标量;待审是反事实干预(移除标量反馈,换信息冗余度门控),检验进步在标量缺席下是否幸存。一个问"哪些被丢弃的决策最有价值",另一个问"如果根本不让 agent 看见标量、只用信息新颖性把关,系统还能不能进步"。前者关注 rejection 这一动作的信息,后者关注 reward signal 的本体替换(scalar frontier → admissible information gain)。两者机制独立,故 refuted=false。

The three-arm design is better than average — the ARM-C placebo cleanly separates "hide the number" from "reward non-redundancy," and the two named falsifiers (best-scalar collapse kills the thesis; ARM-B≈ARM-C deflates the mechanism) are aimed in opposite directions and stated in advance. But it is NOT genuinely discriminating, for three reasons.

(1) The "falsifiable prediction" is partly trivial-by-construction. ARM-B's reward IS a non-redundancy gate, while "coverage" is defined as count of distinct admissible clusters and "redundancy rate" is the complement. So predictions (ii) ARM-B has strictly higher coverage and (iii) lower redundancy than the scalar-only arms are near-tautological — you are testing your own definition, not the world. Only conjunct (i), "best hidden-scalar survives within a small margin of ARM-A," is non-trivial; all discriminating power collapses onto that one comparison.

(2) That single load-bearing comparison is dominated by a knob the author concedes is decisive but cannot canonically pin down. Per the actual paper (EinsteinArena, arXiv:2606.10402v1; Table 1), submissions are mathematical constructions — point configurations (kissing number d=11: 593→604), degree-69 polynomials, circle packings — objects with NO natural metric/embedding. Rotations/permutations leave the scalar invariant but move raw coordinates arbitrarily far; distinct constructions can embed close. So coverage, redundancy, and whether ARM-B's gate even admits the frontier-improving submission are dominated by an arbitrary epsilon/representation choice. Prescribing ">=3 epsilon and >=2 representations" makes this worse, not better: it yields a result grid in which some cell shows "survives" and some "collapses," enabling post-hoc selection rather than a single committed test.

(3) The treatment confounds the very hypotheses it claims to separate. ARM-A rewards moving the scalar; ARM-B causally screens the scalar off from reward and pays only for correct+novel. The paper's actual frontier gains (11 of 12 results) came from INCREMENTAL, iterative, near-redundant submissions building on prior ones — exactly the moves a non-redundancy gate mechanically REJECTS. So if ARM-B's best-scalar collapses, the stated falsifier ("frontier visibility was load-bearing") is indistinguishable from the alternative ("the novelty gate rejected the incremental improvements that produce frontier progress"). The decisive arm produces the same observable collapse under both competing hypotheses, so it cannot adjudicate between them.

Additionally, executability is heavy though secondary: the real result required ~2 months of in-the-wild multi-agent traffic over 12 hard open-math problems with verifier refinement and a forum; running three matched-budget arms plus a defensible meta-verifier embedding for non-embeddable objects is a large, costly re-run, not a minimal experiment.

Net: the prediction is partly trivially-true, the one non-trivial comparison is governed by an admittedly decisive metric knob with no canonical setting (enabling result-agnostic self-rationalization via the sensitivity sweep), and the treatment confounds the two hypotheses on the only load-bearing arm. The experiment does not cleanly distinguish "the scalar was never the engine" from "the novelty gate destroyed the incremental mechanism that drives the scalar." refuted=true.

两种结局都不翻转任何具体决策。(A) 若"集体进步存活/变好"→结论是"标量前沿只是记分牌不是引擎"——这只是 novelty search / MAP-Elites / Goodhart 已有文献(乃至本项目自己的 QD 引擎前提)的复述,没有任何平台运营方(Kaggle、arena、benchmark 维护者)会据此拆掉标量榜:因为可接纳门的核心"信息冗余判定(ii)"恰恰把整个难题偷换成已解——而谁来计算 information-redundancy、那个 meta-verifier 怎么造,正是本项目 MEMORY 里反复点名的真正命门(0 驳回 / LLM 自评几乎永远说 yes / 识别到 c21≡c15 却不下刀)。实验只产出"存活/死亡"这一 bit,不告诉造冗余闸门的团队该怎么造,难点被假设已解。(B) 若"进步死亡"→结论是"标量是承重的",但没人正打算拆榜,确认"该留着"同样改不了任何在投决策。点名:即便是 frameshift 自己造对抗式价值闸门的团队,拿到这个 survives/dies 结论也得不到任何门设计决策——因为问题把闸门的可实现性当前提塞了进去。这正是用户自己在 M3 诊断里判过的 c01/c02:"漂亮但实验空,把有画面感误当新意"。属于"加深对'记分牌≠引擎'这一已知框架的理解",即判据明令归为空话的那一类。
